---
title: "NeSI Scripts"
author: "Holly Gray"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Basecalling

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
#!/bin/bash -e

#SBATCH --account        massey03727
#SBATCH --job-name       dorado
#SBATCH --gpus-per-node  A100:1
#SBATCH --mem            12G
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task  6
#SBATCH --time           24:00:00
#SBATCH --output         dorado_basecaller.out
#SBATCH --error         %x_%j.err

#purge any other modules loaded in the background. This will avoid potential conflicts. Then load the required

module purge
module load Dorado/0.7.3

dorado download --model dna_r10.4.1_e8.2_400bps_sup@v5.0.0

#dorado basecaller <model> <reads> --kit-name <barcode-kit-name> > calls.bam
dorado basecaller --device 'cuda:all' --min-qscore 10 --no-trim sup pod5/ > calls.bam
```

### Demultiplexing

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
#!/bin/bash -e

#SBATCH --account        massey03727
#SBATCH --job-name       dorado_demultiplex
#SBATCH --mem            20G
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task  16
#SBATCH --time           08:00:00
#SBATCH --output         dorado_demultiplex.out
#SBATCH --error         %x_%j.err

#purge any other modules loaded in the background. This will avoid potential conflicts. Then load the required

module purge
module load Dorado/0.7.3

#dorado demux --output-dir <output-dir> --no-classify <input-bam>
dorado demux --kit-name SQK-RBK114-24 --emit-fastq --output-dir Barcodes_fastq calls.bam
```

## Quality control

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
#!/bin/bash -e
#SBATCH --account massey03727
#SBATCH --job-name=filtlong # job name (shows up in the queue)
#SBATCH --output=filtlong.log
#SBATCH --time=24:00:00 # Walltime (HH:MM:SS)
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=4 # number of cpus/threads, stick to 2 for now
#SBATCH --mem=20G            # RAM
#SBATCH --error         %x_%j.err

module purge

module load Filtlong/0.2.0

filtlong --min_length 1000 --keep_percent 90 --target_bases 500000000 <input.fastq> | gzip > <output_QC.fastq.gz>

```

## Assembly

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
#!/bin/bash -e
#SBATCH --account massey03727
#SBATCH --job-name=flye # job name (shows up in the queue)
#SBATCH --output=flye.log
#SBATCH --time=30:00:00 # Walltime (HH:MM:SS)
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=4 # number of cpus/threads, stick to 2 for now
#SBATCH --mem=20G            # RAM
#SBATCH --error         %x_%j.err

module purge

module load Flye/2.9.3-gimkl-2022a-Python-3.11.3

flye --nano-hq output_QC.fastq.gz --genome-size 5m --out-dir <Isolate_ID>

```

## Polishing

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
#!/bin/bash -e
#SBATCH --account=massey03727
#SBATCH --job-name=medaka_test # job name (shows up in the queue)
#SBATCH --output=%x.%j.out
#SBATCH --error=%x.%j.err
#SBATCH --mem=15G            # RAM
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=4    # number of cpus/threads
#SBATCH --time=12:00:00      # Walltime (HH:MM:SS)


# Load necessary modules
module purge
module load medaka/1.11.1-Miniconda3-22.11.1-1
module load BWA/0.7.17-gimkl-2017a
module load SAMtools/1.19-GCC-12.3.0
module load minimap2/2.28-GCC-12.3.0

# Set parameters
input_file="STEC_phage_26Aug24.txt" # Path to the input text file
output_dir="./Medaka"
threads=4

# Ensure the input file exists
if [[ ! -f "$input_file" ]]; then
    echo "Error: Input file '$input_file' not found!"
    exit 1
fi

# Read the text file line by line
while IFS=$'\t' read -r sample_id fasta_file long_read; do
    if [[ -z "$sample_id" || -z "$fasta_file" || -z "$long_read" ]]; then
        echo "Error: Missing data in line: $sample_id, $fasta_file, $long_read"
        continue
    fi

    # Check if the files exist
    if [[ ! -f "$fasta_file" ]]; then
        echo "Error: FASTA file '$fasta_file' not found for sample '$sample_id'!"
        continue
    fi
    if [[ ! -f "$long_read" ]]; then
        echo "Error: Long read file '$long_read' not found for sample '$sample_id'!"
        continue
    fi

    # Index the FASTA file
    echo "Indexing FASTA file: $fasta_file"
    samtools faidx "$fasta_file"

    # Run Medaka consensus
    echo "Running Medaka for sample: $sample_id"
    medaka_consensus -i "$long_read" -d "$fasta_file" -o "${output_dir}/${sample_id}_medaka" -t "$threads"

    # Move final polished assembly
    if [[ -f "${output_dir}/${sample_id}_medaka/consensus.fasta" ]]; then
        mv "${output_dir}/${sample_id}_medaka/consensus.fasta" "${output_dir}/${sample_id}_medaka.fasta"
    else
        echo "Warning: Medaka consensus failed for sample '$sample_id'. No consensus.fasta found."
    fi
done < "$input_file"
```

## Genome assembly statistics

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
#!/bin/bash -e
#SBATCH --account massey03727
#SBATCH --job-name=quast # job name (shows up in the queue)
#SBATCH --output=quast.log
#SBATCH --time=05:00:00 # Walltime (HH:MM:SS)
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=4 # number of cpus/threads, stick to 2 for now
#SBATCH --mem=40G            # RAM
#SBATCH --error         %x_%j.err

module purge

module load QUAST/5.2.0-gimkl-2022a

quast.py *.fasta  -o quast_output
```

## MLST

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
#!/bin/bash -e

#SBATCH --account       massey03727
#SBATCH --job-name      mlst
#SBATCH --time          02:10:00
#SBATCH --mem           8GB
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task 8
#SBATCH --error         %x_%j.err
#SBATCH --output        %x_%j.out

module purge
module load nullarbor/2.0.20191013

mlst --csv --scheme ecoli *.fasta > all_mlst.csv
```

## SBI Typing

The first step in this process is to create a database with the SBI relivant genes and the instertion products to determine the SBI type. This initial script was provided by Aline. 

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
#!/bin/bash -e
#SBATCH --account=massey03727
#SBATCH --job-name=abricate_db # job name (shows up in the queue)
#SBATCH --output=slurmlog/%x.%j.out
#SBATCH --error=%x.%j.err
#SBATCH --mem=8G            # RAM
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=4 # number of cpus/threads, stick to 2 for now
#SBATCH --time=05:00:00 # Walltime (HH:MM:SS)

module purge
module load ABRicate/1.0.0-GCC-11.3.0-Perl-5.34.1

# make blast db
# need to put your db inside a subdirectory
mkdir /nesi/nobackup/massey03727/Objective_2_STEC/final_polished_assemblies/db/SBI_abricate_db

cd /nesi/nobackup/massey03727/Objective_2_STEC/final_polished_assemblies/db/

# need to name your fasta file as "sequences"
# using my other database as an example here
# copy CampyPlasmidtyping_abricate_20240710.fasta into CampyPlasmidtyping_abricate_20240710 directory, and rename fasta to 'sequences'
cp insertionProducts.fa /nesi/nobackup/massey03727/Objective_2_STEC/final_polished_assemblies/db/SBI_abricate_db/insertionProducts.fa

cd /nesi/nobackup/massey03727/Objective_2_STEC/final_polished_assemblies/db/SBI_abricate_db
mv insertionProducts.fa sequences

# make database
# the -title flag doesn't actually seem to do anything - then db name used in abricate is actually the directory name
makeblastdb -in sequences -title test -dbtype nucl -hash_index

# view your available databases
abricate --datadir /nesi/nobackup/massey03727/Objective_2_STEC/final_polished_assemblies/db/ --list

# run abricate - db should be one of the ones listed in the above command
#example with my plasmid database showing how I am going to use one isolate against the database I made
#cd /nesi/nobackup/massey03742/Linda_database/
#abricate --datadir /nesi/nobackup/massey03742/Linda_database/db/ --db CampyPlasmidtyping_abricate_20240710 SC0083_Contig_1_743.574_Circ.fasta

```

### Running Abricate with custom database

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
#!/bin/bash -e
#SBATCH --account massey03727
#SBATCH --job-name=abricate # job name (shows up in the queue)
#SBATCH --output=abricate.log
#SBATCH --time=02:00:00 # Walltime (HH:MM:SS)
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=4 # number of cpus/threads, stick to 2 for now
#SBATCH --mem=20G            # RAM
#SBATCH --error         %x_%j.err

module purge

module load ABRicate/1.0.0-GCC-11.3.0-Perl-5.34.1

abricate --datadir /nesi/nobackup/massey03727/Objective_2_STEC/final_polished_assemblies/db/ --db SBI_abricate_db ../139_fasta_files/*.fasta > results.tab

abricate --summary results.tab > summary.tab

```

## Genome annotation

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
#!/bin/bash -e
#SBATCH --account massey03727
#SBATCH --job-name=prokka # job name (shows up in the queue)
#SBATCH --output=prokka.log
#SBATCH --time=24:00:00 # Walltime (HH:MM:SS)
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=16 # number of cpus/threads, stick to 2 for now
#SBATCH --mem=40G            # RAM
#SBATCH --error         %x_%j.err

module purge

module load prokka/1.14.5-GCC-11.3.0

mkdir -p prokka_results
for F in *.fasta; do
  N=$(basename "$F" .fasta)
  prokka --genus Escherichia --cpus 16 --locustag "$N" --outdir prokka_results/"$N" --prefix "$N" "$F"
done
```


```{bash, eval=FALSE}

```


```{bash, eval=FALSE}

```


```{bash, eval=FALSE}

```
